Extend-not-duplicate confirmed: exactly ONE TopologySurface enum in mission_runtime/artifacts.py:23 with PRIMARY/COORD/LANE/CONSOLIDATED/TEMP; the 3 new members ADDED to the existing enum (COORD replaces PLACEMENT), no bare Surface enum in mission_runtime. review.reference: APPROVE (reviewer-renata, independent). Behaviour-free campsite split verified. Oracle test_transaction.py byte-identical to base (empty diff) and 35/35 green unchanged. AST diff: every moved symbol identical to base except the 3 legacy predicates, which only swap inline meta-read for the new _load_mission_meta helper (T042 fold) - verified behaviour-identical (same feature_dir, same on_malformed=none; runtime read count unchanged, so no read-timing risk). Re-exports keep from-transaction imports and transaction.X monkeypatch anchors resolving; _acquire_locked calls legacy helpers as bare globals. Dead threading.local sentinel had zero repo refs; import threading removed. 6 flattened refs (R-005) intact (tx 2 + legacy_resolution 4). Arch allow-list append for legacy_resolution.py is the sanctioned-constructor relocation (code-follows-move). Gates: ruff clean, mypy clean under real gate mypy --strict src/specify_cli src/charter src/doctrine (1067 files, 0 issues; subset Any-returns are the documented follow_imports=skip artifact, identical in base), zero new suppressions. 119 downstream consumer tests green. C10 (transaction.py <=1000) DEFERRED TO WP09: WP08 lands at 1016 LOC - the confined-write orchestration (_resolve/_write/_unlink, 92 LOC) cannot extract behaviour-free because the frozen oracle monkeypatches transaction._resolve_confined_artifact_path and counts a fixed 3 resolve calls through the transaction namespace; reaching ~924 needs a DI seam that is behaviour-CHANGING (WP09 territory, per orchestrator decision). Genuine oracle-coupling constraint, not laziness.

review.reference: APPROVED (reviewer-renata, independent). NI-2 terminal-set guard correct: enforce_negative_invariants keys on TERMINAL_INVARIANT_RESULTS={confirmed_absent,still_present,verification_error}; deferred_to_consolidation handled in a separate branch (not frozen), preserving C6 re-judgeability. NFR-004/C3 preservation intact (test_ni2_recorded_terminal_preserved_verbatim re-runs against a different surface, asserts verbatim). NI-5 ordering correct: overall_verdict checks fail>pending>pass_pending_consolidation>pass; pending/still_present dominate deferral (pinned); clean terminal-only matrix still passes. C2 round-trip: to_dict omits null/legacy defaults, from_dict restores via dataclass defaults (test passes). NI-1 validation enforces recorded-terminal provenance + anti-phantom origin; pending/deferred exempt (defensible: no surface to attribute). NI-3/C9 deferral wired into accept gate via context; NI-7 disclosure emitted as skipped_check. S105 suppression on VERDICT_PASS_PENDING_CONSOLIDATION: genuine false positive (verdict literal contains 'pass', not a secret), narrow single-line, inline-justified, and the value must match the data-model vocabulary so renaming is worse -- ACCEPTED per charter narrow-suppression clause. 23/23 provenance tests pass; re-pins (result-vocab + **_kw stub) are correct API-expansion consequences, not weakening. ruff clean, complexity<=15, matrix.py mypy-clean; the 6 file-by-file no-any-return hits are the documented follow_imports=skip artifact on non-WP04 lines and clear under whole-package mypy. #2882 single-home: scaffolder now home-aware -- closes the finalize/scaffold divergence vector WP04 owns (C8/FR-010); residual coord commit-router placement belongs to the structural write-placement mission (#2841), not a WP04 gap. Leeway on gates_core.py + mission_finalize.py documented.

ACTION REQUIRED:
1. NFR-002 harness (tests/integration/test_tool_artifact_owner.py::test_nfr002_enrolled_path_never_observes_a_third_state) is VACUOUS for the property it must guard. Its kill arm sleeps 30s BEFORE the write (kill always lands during sleep, write never starts -> pre) and its complete arm runs the full write to completion before the parent reads (-> post). NO trial ever kills DURING the temp-write/os.replace. Therefore the assert data in (pre,post) is a tautology that holds for ANY write implementation, atomic or not: a future regression replacing the atomic temp+os.replace (atomic_write.py:131) with a non-atomic in-place write would leave this test GREEN. The both-outcomes floor (completed>=1, killed_before>=1) is satisfied trivially by trial%2 and does NOT establish kill-atomicity.
2. This contradicts the harness docstring which claims to follow tests/integration/test_intake_atomic_writes.py. That reference idiom races a RANDOM 0-1ms delay SIGKILL against an IMMEDIATE child write so some kills land mid-write; its own comment warns: if the OS always finishes before the SIGKILL we are not really testing atomicity. WP09 fell into exactly that trap by construction.
3. FIX (product code unchanged): adopt the reference idiom. Child writes immediately (no pre-write sleep); parent sleeps a small random delay then SIGKILLs, racing the write. Keep the both-outcomes floor (>=1 pre, >=1 post) so the race demonstrably lands kills on both sides of the replace. Then the assert can actually fail on a non-atomic regression.

MINOR (non-blocking, address if touching): tests/merge/test_merge_done_recording.py aliases the retired name restore_generated_artifact_snapshots as _restore_final_bookkeeping_snapshots. Test-only, outside C4b src/ scope, semantics-preserving, but keeps a retired name alive; prefer renaming the local usages.

review.reference:
APPROVE. Focused re-verification of the single prior rejection axis (vacuous NFR-002 harness) — RESOLVED. Product code UNCHANGED (2fc18517a touches only 2 test files; transaction.py still 963 LOC; oracle test_transaction.py git-diff empty, 35/35). C4b symbols still absent from src/.

Harness now genuinely races the kill against the write: child writes the ~1 MiB post payload IMMEDIATELY (no pre-write sleep); parent sleeps rng.uniform(0, max(3x write_dur, 1ms)) then SIGKILLs, so kills land mid temp-write / mid os.replace. Falsifiability confirmed by reasoning: the atomic temp+os.replace impl leaves target = pre (tmp orphaned) or post, never partial; a non-atomic in-place regression killed mid-loop would leave a truncated post-prefix (len != 1MiB, content != pre and != post) tripping assert data in (pre,post). Empirical evidence across 3 runs: killed/survived = 119/81, 115/85, 132/68 (healthy both-outcomes split, NOT a trial%2 tautology); mid-write temp orphans = 50, 51, 64 per 200 trials = ~25% of SIGKILLs landing DURING the temp-write phase. The both-outcomes floor (killed>=1 AND completed>=1) now fails LOUDLY on a vacuous run rather than passing silently. 11/11 tool-artifact-owner tests pass; oracle+merge 69/69 pass. Minor prior nit also resolved: the retired name _restore_final_bookkeeping_snapshots is no longer aliased in test_merge_done_recording.py (import and both call sites now use canonical restore_generated_artifact_snapshots; tests green). Meets NFR-002 / DIRECTIVE_041.
